observation.page_a: AFC Bournemouth 1-1 Manchester City, venue: Vitality Stadium
observation.page_b: Manchester City 3-1 AFC Bournemouth, venue: Etihad Stadium
observation.implication: same slug + same route code + different hash ID = different fixtures with different home/away

model.slug: display/SEO only — not authoritative for home/away orientation
model.route_code: matchup route / team-pair entry point — NOT unique fixture identity
model.hash_id: fixture selector within the route — determines which concrete fixture is displayed
model.canonical_identity: atomic tuple = {route_code, hash_id} — must be preserved as unit

l2_prohibitions: rewrite slug based on expected home/away
treat route_code alone as unique fixture identity
treat hash_id alone as unique fixture identity
use historical enriched URL as primary L2 URL source
guess route_code when L1 has not provided one

contract.l2_must_consume: canonical_detail_url as atomic unit; do not decompose and rewrite components
contract.l2_must_validate: observed home/away/date after fetch; do not assume orientation from URL

builder_assessment.issue_1: detail ID as route code fallback — violates model (route_code ≠ detail_id)
builder_assessment.issue_2: slug rewriting based on expected home/away — violates model (slug not authoritative)
builder_assessment.issue_3: route_code guessing when historical URL unavailable — violates model
builder_assessment.recommendation: deprecate buildCorrectedFotmobDetailUrl() for primary L2 use; use only when no L1 canonical URL exists AND mark as provisional

adg32_38_explained: L1 API provided route_code + hash_id for reverse fixture; correct-orientation hash_id was not discovered
recommended_next_step: ADG41: implement canonical URL atomic handoff contract; block L2 URL rewriting; do not raw write
